title: Arkanian Legacy
content:

[[Omonoth system/Legends|Omonoth system]][[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]] |retired= |battles=[[First Battle of Omonoth]] |affiliation=The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania |navy= |fleet= |owners=Arkoh Adasca |captains= |crewmembers=*[[Suprin]][[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 18]] *[[Eejee Vamm]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Everything that is good in Arkanian culture is right here.|Arkoh Adasca|[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]]}} The '''''Arkanian Legacy''''' was a massive [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] that served as the mobile headquarters of [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania]], also known as Adascorp, in the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Originally intended as a means of moving corporate operations out of the fire of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], it later evolved into a center of [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] culture, including a [[University/Legends|university]], art [[Museum/Legends|museum]] and concert hall, along with extensive medical and scientific laboratories. During the Mandalorian Wars, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' also became the site of Adascorp [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|CEO]] [[Arkoh Adasca]]'s ploy for [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] prominence, as he attempted to sell the destructive power of the massive [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorth]] [[Species/Legends|species]] to the highest bidder, effectively choosing the winner of the war. As such, Adasca invited representatives from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] and [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] to the ''Arkanian Legacy'', hosting an auction of sorts among the parties fighting the war. However, with control of the galaxy's fate within his grasp, Adasca's plan came apart when the scientist he was using to control the exogorths, [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Prison/Legends|prisoner]] [[Gorman Vandrayk]], set the massive space slugs on the ''Arkanian Legacy'' in the midst of a [[First Battle of Omonoth|battle]] that had broken out on the ship. Adasca was [[Death/Legends|killed]], and the remains of the ship were left irreparably damaged and listing in contested space on the front lines of the Mandalorian Wars. ==Description== {{Quote|During the Sith War, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' was simply intended to be a means of moving the corporate headquarters out of harm's way. But it's become much more. A medical research center. A university. Even a domed observatory for concerts under the stars.|Arkoh Adasca|}} Initially created as the mobile corporate base of [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania]], better known as Adascorp, the colossal [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] ''Arkanian Legacy'' housed up to 104,079 people and also served as a research [[Medcenter/Legends|hospital]], [[arboretum]], conservatory, one of the largest art [[Museum/Legends|museums]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], a [[University/Legends|university]] and a domed observatory that doubled as a concert venue. [[File:Arkanian_Legacy_atrium.png|thumb|left|250px|The ''Arkanian Legacy'' atrium]] Employing a skilled crew of 5,000, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' was defended by an armament of four light and 12 medium [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, as well as 24 [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and eight [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]], not including [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Arkoh Adasca]]'s private vessel. In addition, various research, transport, passenger and visitor craft were usually housed aboard the ''Arkanian Legacy'' at any one time. Equipped with a [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputer]] and a class 2 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] with a class 8 backup, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' could hold up to 10,000 tons of cargo, 100,000 passengers, and two years' worth of consumables. Among its other features, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' contained a crow's nest with dozens of [[Viewscreen/Legends|holoscreens]], which Adasca's [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetically]]-enhanced [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] assistant, [[Eejee Vamm]], used as a workstation. From there, Vamm had access to video recordings and broadcasts from all over the galaxy, and had the ability to run retinal scans on people within those recordings.[[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]] In addition, the crow's nest had access to hundreds of different security cameras within the ''Arkanian Legacy'' itself. The ''Arkanian Legacy''{{'s}} hospital was a fully-outfitted unit, including quarantine care centers and extensive scientific laboratories. True to Adascorp's mission, much medical and biomechanical research was carried out on the ship, both in the laboratories and in the ''Arkanian Legacy''{{'s}} multiple libraries. The center of the ship's opulent facilities was its wide-open atrium, anchored in the center by a massive fountain. The atrium connected to a series of ornately outfitted personal quarters that were often used to house important guests. For less well-appointed guests, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' also possessed its own [[Prison/Legends|brig]], guarded by a battalion of security personnel. ==History== ===Plotting for the galaxy=== {{Quote|Lord Adasca, this is Eejee Vamm. I have what you've been looking for…|Eejee Vamm, after using the ''Arkanian Legacy''{{'s}} crow's nest to find Gorman Vandrayk|[[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]]}} [[File:Deep_space_refueling.jpg|thumb|right|350px|The ''Arkanian Legacy'' at a refueling station after capturing Gorman Vandrayk]] First conceived of during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] as a method for Adascorp to keep its headquarters out of harm's way, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' was initially the project of Adasca's grandfather [[Argaloh Adasca|Argaloh]], and was eventually completed by Argaloh's son [[Alok Adasca|Alok]]. The ''Arkanian Legacy'' was finally launched at the ceremony where Arkoh Adasca, Alok's son and the eighth Lord of the [[House of Adasca]], came into his inheritance as the head of Adascorp, [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] largest [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineering]] firm. The scion of a great industrial dynasty, Adasca became immensely popular among pureblood [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanians]] as the [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|Chief Executive Officer]] of Adascorp, but he had grander ambitions in mind than just being the head of a major corporation. During the [[Time/Legends|time]] of his grandfather's stewardship of the company, a brilliant [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] by the name of [[Gorman Vandrayk]] had discovered how to contact and control the minds of the [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorths]], a species of massive and mysterious [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Slug (creature)/Legends|slug]] found in many disparate regions of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Realizing the incredible destructive power of the beasts, Argaloh planned to weaponize the exogorths, but a horrified Vandrayk went on the run and took his indispensable knowledge with him.[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]] By [[3963 BBY]], the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] had become engulfed in the [[Mandalorian Wars]], fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior clans. Remembering his grandfather's vision for the exogorths, Adasca formulated a plan that could possibly turn Adascorp into a galactic power. The company had discovered a host of dormant exogorths near the dying [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Omonoth]], and Adasca figured that if Adascorp could take control of the beasts, he could sell their services to the highest bidder and effectively choose the winner of the war—but, as he would retain operational control of the exogorths, whichever side he chose would be forced to pay him tribute. As grand as Adasca's ambitions were, his plan had no chance of success without Vandrayk, as Adascorp was unable to take control of the exogorths without his knowledge. Adasca made tracking Vandrayk down a high priority, and enlisted his cybernetic aide Eejee Vamm to use the observational power of the ''Arkanian Legacy''{{'s}} holoscreen crow's nest to peruse the galaxy for him. Vamm eventually recognized Vandrayk from a recording on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]], and a retinal scan confirmed the runaway scientist's identity. The Duros immediately messaged Lord Adasca, confirming that he had what the head of Adascorp had been looking for. It was a while longer before Adasca could get Vandrayk onto the ''Arkanian Legacy'', but he managed to get his hands on the elderly Offshoot when he was forced to return to [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] to treat an illness. With the help of Vandrayk's friend [[Jarael]], whom Adasca manipulated into trusting him, Adasca learned of the location of Vandrayk's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']], and swiftly found and [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractored]] it onto the ''Arkanian Legacy''. After finally getting his hands on Gorman Vandrayk, Adasca decided that the [[Time/Legends|time]] was right to make his grab for power. The ''Arkanian Legacy'' was filled to capacity—with most of the 100,000-plus aboard being pureblood [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanians]]—but as the ship made its long journey to the [[Omonoth system/Legends|Omonoth system]], Adasca began quietly transferring residents who were not in on his plan to other assignments off the ship. First, the ship stopped at a deep-space refueling station, where the sickly Vandrayk was rushed to the on-ship hospital, despite his Mandalorian friend [[Demagol|Rohlan Dyre]] attacking several security guards. However, Adasca managed to pacify Dyre by granting the curious Mandalorian access to the ship's research facilities, eliminating another potential obstacle. ===A game of thrones=== {{Quote|Don't you care what happens to the people?''"
"''You didn't care what happened to ''mine,'' Jedi! Your war with the Mandalorians is soon to engulf Arkania—a war we had no role in starting! Well, I care what happens to them. I choose to place Arkania ''above'' this conflict—and this is how!|Alek and Arkoh Adasca|}} As the ''Arkanian Legacy'' continued its voyage to Omonoth, Adasca and his assistant Vamm busied themselves with sending out invitations to representatives from the Galactic Republic, Mandalorians and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Once the ship reached its destination, the visitors begin to arrive: a small contingent of Republic survivors from the [[Battle of Serroco]], led by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Saul Karath]]; [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], leader of the Mandalorians, and a large complement of warriors; and [[Darth Malak|Alek]], the second-in-command of the Jedi Order's [[Revanchist]] faction that advocated [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] intervention in the Mandalorian Wars. They were soon joined by a surprise arrival, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lucien Draay]], an old acquaintance of Adasca's and a member of the secret [[Jedi Covenant]] group of [[Jedi Seer|seers]], who had deduced that Adasca was up to something. Refusing to accommodate a circumstance he had not planned for, Adasca had Draay [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] and locked in the ship's brig with Republic prisoner [[Zayne Carrick]], Draay's former Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] who had been on the run from the Covenant after being accused of a [[Padawan Massacre|crime]] he did not commit.[[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]] [[File:RIPArkanianLegacy.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Exogorths rip through the hull of the ''Arkanian Legacy''.]] Once all the interested parties were aboard, Adasca took his seat in the ''Arkanian Legacy''{{'s}} atrium and began overseeing a chaotic auction of sorts. Although the factions were technically under a flag of truce, that did not prevent Alek from attempting to attack the Mandalore, nearly causing an armed confrontation between the Republic and Mandalorian representatives. After demonstrating the exogorths' destructive power, Adasca began to field offers—most tantalizing being the Mandalorians' promise to make Adasca their sole weaponsmith and chief man of science, which would grant him enormous power and opportunity in a Mandalorian-dominated galaxy. However, Carrick and Draay, normally enemies, began plotting from the brig to escape and derail Adasca's plans. Together, they overpowered their guards, reclaimed Draay's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and cut themselves free. After venturing to the ship's crow's nest and discovering what was happening through the ship's security cameras, they met up with [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Carth Onasi]], a member of the Republic detachment and an acquaintance of Carrick's. After retrieving his lightsaber from Onasi, Carrick got a spare set of Mandalorian armor from Rohlan Dyre and staged an fight with Onasi on the floor in front of Adasca, using the diversion to free Jarael—whom Adasca was using as a hostage to compel Vandrayk to continue his work on the exogorths—and confront the Adascorp CEO. With a full-fledged [[First Battle of Omonoth|battle]] underway between Adasca's security force and the Jedi and Republic representatives, the Mandalorian contingent retreated to the Mandalore's private shuttle. Adasca was unable to reach Vamm for aid—little did he know, the Duros, along with the Arkanian [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Suprin]], had been murdered by Dyre before the firefight broke out. Vandrayk, who had been confined to an orbital exogorth control station, received word that Jarael was free, and immediately set the monstrous beasts on Adasca. After transmitting a mocking message to the Lord of House Adasca, Vandrayk ordered the exogorths to attack the ''Arkanian Legacy'' itself. Carrick, Jarael and the Republic and Jedi contingents managed to escape in the chaos, leaving Arkoh Adasca to die with his plans and his headquarters. After the exogorths ripped through the ship, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' was left derelict and listing in contested space. In the midst of the Mandalorian Wars, Republic forces were unable to determine whether any were left alive on the ship, and ultimately decided against launching a rescue and recovery mission. The ship was ultimately ruled unsalvageable by the Galactic Republic [[Ministry of Defense|Defense Ministry]].{{KOTORbackups|24}} Officially, the destruction of the ''Arkanian Legacy'' was ascribed to a Mandalorian raid that took Adasca's life—as Adascorp was an important corporate citizen of the Republic, all mention of Adasca's plots and what really happened on the ''Arkanian Legacy'' was kept out of public knowledge.{{KOTORbackups|22}} For his part, Saul Karath was solely credited with saving the Republic forces in the battle and turning back the Mandalorian raid, greatly elevating his public profile.[[Knights of the Old Republic 49|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 49]] Later in the war, a Republic force led by Karath and Alek managed to [[Second Battle of Omonoth|retake]] Omonoth long enough to salvage the remnants of the ''Arkanian Legacy''. Among other things, the Republic outfit recovered the mutilated corpse of Eejee Vamm in one of the ship's labs.[[Knights of the Old Republic 47|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 47]] ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|This vessel was my grandfather's great project. My father saw it completed.|Arkoh Adasca|}} [[File:Adasca_HS.png|thumb|right|200px|Arkoh Adasca]] Conceived of by his grandfather Argaloh, the ''Arkanian Legacy'' passed to Arkoh Adasca upon his ascension to the stewardship of Adascorp. As the company's CEO, Adasca oversaw everything on Adascorp's mobile headquarters, using the hundreds of security cameras stationed throughout the ship to keep track of all events that took place on the ''Arkanian Legacy'' and his wider information network to observe much of what occurred in the galaxy beyond. Adasca's singleminded focus was to find Gorman Vandrayk and put his plan for galactic domination in action, and his search brought a tremendous amount of data into the ''Arkanian Legacy'', forcing him to employ a cybernetically-augmented Duros aide, Eejee Vamm, to help manage all the information. Although Vamm was one of the only non-pureblooded Arkanians among the well over 100,000 crew and passengers aboard the ship at its peak, he was unflinchingly loyal to Adasca, as were the myriad doctors, scientists and researchers on the ship, led by the Arkanian Doctor Suprin. Although almost all the regular inhabitants of the ''Arkanian Legacy'' were Arkanians, not all of them were privy to Adasca's plans, and the CEO of Adascorp transferred those who were not in the know off the ship on the journey to Omonoth. Among the Arkanians left on the ship were Adasca's armored security team, who wielded [[Stun baton/Legends|stun batons]] and were aided by a contingent of [[HK-24 series assassin droid|HK-24 assassin droids]]. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Arkanian Legacy'' first briefly appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]], an issue of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2007]]. Although the ship's first appearance only showed its holographic crow's nest, the rest of the ship made its first appearance several issues later, in [[Knights of the Old Republic 16|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 16]]. The ''Arkanian Legacy'' went on to be the primary setting for the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Nights of Anger|''Nights of Anger'']] and [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Daze of Hate|''Daze of Hate'']] story arcs. The ship was designed by artist [[Brian Ching]], who "spent a lot of time" in developing the ship's appearance according to Miller.{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor16.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #16''}} During its appearances in ''Knights of the Old Republic'', the ship was also illustrated by [[Harvey Tolibao]] and [[Bong Dazo]]. The ''Arkanian Legacy'' and the story that takes place aboard it was at least partially inspired by [[Archie Goodwin]]'s depiction of [[The Wheel/Legends|The Wheel]] during the original [[Marvel Comics]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] comic run,{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor17.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #17''}} and Miller devised the ship's capacity from that of [[Wikipedia:Neyland Stadium|Neyland Stadium]], the football stadium for the [[Wikipedia:University of Tennessee|University of Tennessee]],{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?t=16909&postdays=0&postorder=asc&start=30|thread=KNIGHTS OF THE OLD REPUBLIC #50 -- My only friend, the end!|user=[[John Jackson Miller]]|date=2010-02-27|time=15:15|quote=Peak capacity of Arkanian Legacy in the Handbook was the same as Neyland Stadium -- at least until they added seats again...}} Miller's alma mater.{{WebCite|url=https://cci.utk.edu/blog/2020/03/19/john-jackson-miller-editor-to-sci-fi-writer|text=John Jackson Miller: From Daily Beacon Editor to Star Fantasy Writer|date=2020-03-19|work=cci.utk.edu|archiveurl=https://archive.is/Di2H8}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Arkanian Legacy}}
